name: Grilled Chicken Quesadillas
recipeIngredient: 1 Whole chicken breast, boneless and skinless
2 cups Grated cheddar cheese (mild or sharp)
1 cup Grated Chihuahua cheese or Monterey Jack cheese
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 20 minutes Cooking time: 10 minutes
[Note: the serving size (24) is the number of pieces you will get -- not necessarily how many people it will feed.]
1. Heat grill or broiler and cook chicken until done (approximately 5 minutes on each side). Cool to room temperature and shred into 1/4-inch pieces.
2. Over a gas burner or under the broiler, char the poblano pepper until black all over, rotating as necessary. Place the pepper in a bag and seal it. After about 15 minutes, remove the pepper from the bag and discard the charred skin, seeds, and stem. Cut the pepper into 1/2-inch strips.
3. Place four tortillas on a table. Top each with 3/4 cup of cheese, a pinch of chili powder, half a cup of shredded chicken, and the poblano strips. Cover with remaining tortillas.
4. Heat an 8-inch or larger skillet (dry, no oil) over medium-high heat. Place one quesadilla at a time in the skillet and cook until golden brown (about 1 minute). Turn over and cook another minute.
5. Let cool slightly before cutting into six wedges. Serve with salsa or guacamole if desired.
